questions.prompt: Your chatbot stuffs a 60K-token knowledge base into every call. Bill is 5× budget. Best fix?

choices.text: Move to RAG. Embed the knowledge base, retrieve only the 2–5K tokens relevant to the user's question, and pass just that. Drops input by ~95% with no quality loss for most lookups. Stack with prompt caching on the system prompt for further savings.

questions.explanation: Stuffing the full corpus is the most common Claude cost mistake. RAG retrieves only the relevant chunks per query — typically 1–5% of the corpus. Cost drops dramatically and quality usually improves because Claude isn't distracted by irrelevant context. Module 4 covers RAG in depth.

questions.prompt: Roughly how big is 1,000 tokens in English text?

choices.text: About 750 words — roughly 1.5 single-spaced pages. 1 token ≈ 4 characters of English ≈ 0.75 words. Use this when you sketch costs on a napkin.

questions.explanation: The standard rule of thumb: 1 token ≈ 4 chars of English text ≈ 0.75 words. So 1,000 tokens is about 750 words or 1.5 single-spaced pages, and 200K tokens (Claude's context window) is roughly a 250-page book. Always validate with the token-counting endpoint for real numbers.
